page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease: 156 alterations and 505 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Curcumin and salidroside can improve the course of COPD by regulating blood pressure, inflammation, and the coagulation pathway.
Exploration of quantitative-effectiveness association between acupuncture temporal parameters and stable chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Micronutrient supplementation, such as vitamin D alone and compound nutrients, has improved effect on the lung function of patients with COPD.
Tai chi might be a potential alternative therapy to improve FEV1, 6WMD, anxiety, and quality of life for patients with COPD.
Wuqinxi demonstrated the greatest benefits in improving pulmonary function and exercise capacity.
A growing body of evidence supports qigong and tai chi performed as qigong as valid complementary or alternative therapeutic exercises.
Acupuncture therapy improves health-related quality of life in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
C-reactive protein level predicts mortality in COPD.
Effectiveness of Tai Chi for health promotion for adults with health conditions: a scoping review of Meta-analyses.
People with COPD may obtain benefit from practicing Tai Chi.
Qigong can improve COPD patients in lung function, exercise capacity and quality of life who were in the stable stage.
Qigong can improve the self-rating depression scale and self-rating anxiety scale scores of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease patients.
Tai Chi was beneficial for chronic conditions-cancer, osteoarthritis, heart failure and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Whole-body vibration seems to benefit subjects with COPD by improving their functional exercise capacity, without producing adverse effects.
Cryptotanshinone reverses corticosteroid insensitivity by inhibition of phosphoinositide-3-Kinase-δ in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Mulberry (Morus alba l.) leaf improves arachidonic acid metabolism disorder in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Acupuncture could improve the exercise tolerance in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease at stable phase, and shorten the onset time of aerobic exercise.
A combination of N-acetyl cysteine and propolis attenuates oxidative-inflammatory parameters during COPD exacerbation.
A high dietary intake of vitamin C, or foods rich in this vitamin, may prevent COPD.
A multistrain probiotic improves handgrip strength and functional capacity in patients with COPD.
A single application of LEDT minimizes muscle fatigue and increases isometric endurance time.
A traditional Chinese herbal formula containing Angelica and Rehmannia improves symptoms in partients with COPD.
A whole-body vibration program resulted in significant improvements in exercise capacity in subjects with mild to severe COPD.
Acupressure may lesson chronic shortness of breath and depression in persons with COPD.
Acupuncture and acupressure result in clinically significant improvements in quality of life in patients with chronic obstructive asthma.
Acupuncture contributes to a reduction of COPD-related dyspnea following exercise.
Acupuncture is well tolerated and therapeutic in treating acute exacerbation of COPD.
Acute effects of fine particulate matter constituents on cardiopulmonary function in a panel of COPD patients.
Add-on therapy with Pelargonium sidoides extract led to an improvement in health related quality of life in patients with COPD.
Addition of vitamin B12 to exercise training improves cycle ergometer endurance in advanced COPD patients.
Adjunctive treatment with Rhodiola crenulata in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Air pollution was associated with higher incidence of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Ambient air pollution was associated with lower lung function and increased COPD prevalence in this large study.
Application of PBMT and magnetic field increased the number of steps during the 6MST and decreased the sensation of dyspnea and lower limb fatigue in patients with COPD.
Ascorbate attenuates cycling exercise-induced neuromuscular fatigue.
Association between ambient air pollution and mortality from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Association between E-cigarette use and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease by smoking status.
Association between urinary phthalate metabolites and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Benefits of whole body vibration training in patients hospitalised for COPD exacerbations.

Concomitant therapy with cineole, the main constituent of eucalyptus oil, reduces exacerbations as well as dyspnea (shortness of breath) and improves lung function and health status in patients with COPD.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Effectively zero — airflow obstruction is irreversible by definition in COPD; FEV1 decline continues at 30-60 mL/year on standard care
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Smoking cessation slows FEV1 decline to near-normal aging rate (the only intervention that does this); lung volume reduction surgery (LVRS) improves FEV1 15-20% in selected emphysema patients; 52% of COPD patients achieve clinically meaningful improvement after pulmonary rehabilitation
remission.gap_size: Large for disease modification (smoking cessation the only real lever); moderate for functional outcomes (pulmonary rehab underused)
remission.barrier_detail: Biological limit: structural lung damage is not reversible with any current pharmacotherapy. Practice gap: pulmonary rehabilitation achieves clinically meaningful improvement in 52% of patients but fewer than 2% of eligible COPD patients in the US receive it. 26% of patients with a COPD diagnosis have no evidence of airflow obstruction on spirometry (misdiagnosed, overtreated)
remission.last_soc_change: 2024 — GOLD report added dupilumab for Type 2 inflammation subgroup (eosinophilic COPD); triple therapy (LABA/LAMA/ICS) endpoint data (IMPACT, ETHOS trials 2018-2020); LABA/LAMA dual bronchodilation as backbone unchanged since ~2006
remission.outdated_treatments: ICS monotherapy without LABA/LAMA in non-eosinophilic COPD (increases pneumonia risk, no mortality benefit); mucolytic agents (e.g., NAC) at standard doses (high-dose emerging evidence, but standard dose RCTs largely negative); long-term oral corticosteroids in stable COPD
remission.adjunctive_emerging: High-dose NAC (1800 mg/day vs standard 600 mg) showing FEV1 benefit in Chinese cohorts; endobronchial valve placement (FDA approved, Zephyr valve) for hyperinflation in selected patients; airway clearance devices
remission.frontier_signal: Dupilumab in eosinophilic COPD (BOREAS/NOTUS trials positive, 2023-2024); tezepelumab Phase 3 in COPD; lung regeneration approaches; stem cell therapy (early Phase 1); senolytic therapy (targeting lung senescent cells — navitoclax, dasatinib+quercetin)
